summary: Build the harness reference graph ‚Äî parse a declared edge field in `temper.toml`, assemble a directed graph over the corpus, and flag any route resolving to no artifact.

per.section: The harness is a graph too ‚Äî and references are declared edges

new.description: The harness reference graph (`specs/45-governance.md`). Read a declared edge field from each artifact's `Features` (surfaced via the `extra` catch-all, `extract.rs`) into edges (source id ‚Üí target name), assemble the directed graph over the whole corpus (nodes = artifacts across kinds), and emit a `Diagnostic` for any edge whose target names no artifact ‚Äî the referential decidable check (`specs/10-contracts.md`, the referential primitive: a reference resolves *over a declared syntax*, never prose-grep). Plus `admissibility`: a declared edge relationship must name its reference field + target kind (the reference syntax the referential clause requires).

edit.description: Add `pub mod graph;` ‚Äî the foundation entry owns the new module declaration (no other entry co-edits `lib.rs`).

Parse a declared edge relationship from `temper.toml` into the `AuthorLayer` ‚Äî the reference field name, its owning kind, and the target kind ‚Äî the declared reference syntax the harness graph is built from (`(skill-ref-syntax)` RESOLVED: a declared field, never grepped prose). Fold malformations into a single `ComposeError` the way `parse_count`/`parse_membership` do.

In the `Check` arm, after the roster tier and gated on the `temper.toml` layer being present, build the harness graph over the by-kind `Features` and extend the diagnostic stream with `graph::check` (+ its admissibility). The floor-only (no `temper.toml`) path stays byte-for-byte unchanged.

tests.asserts: A `temper.toml` declaring a `routes_to` edge from rules to skills: a rule whose `routes_to` names a real skill is clean; one naming an absent skill trips a `required` route-resolution diagnostic and fails the run; absent `temper.toml`, no graph runs.

acceptance: `temper check` over a corpus with a `temper.toml`-declared edge field builds the harness reference graph and reports any declared route that resolves to no artifact ‚Äî a dangling route fails the run, a resolving one is clean; the floor-only (no `temper.toml`) path is unchanged.
notes: Foundation for the graph scope ‚Äî `degree`/`acyclic` (`45-governance.md`) follow next tick once this lands. Edges are declared fields, never grepped prose (`(skill-ref-syntax)` RESOLVED); route-resolution is the referential primitive (`10-contracts.md`). The `temper.toml` edge-declaration shape is code's within the kind-declares-relationships intent (`15-kinds.md`). Harness graph only; the `spec` references-resolve clause is the separate spec.toml-parked frontier.

summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: embed `contracts/spec.toml`, project `ws.specs` via `spec_features`, and run admissibility + `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds it via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it in a clean commit

edit.description: Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features via `extract::spec_features`, parse the spec contract, and extend the diagnostic stream with `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them.

tests.asserts: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract ‚Äî the over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity and the run still exits 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract ‚Äî flagging the over-length specs at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: needs SPEC-FEATURES (the extractor, shipped ‚Äî `extract::spec_features` on disk) plus a human to commit the untracked `contracts/spec.toml`. Runs only spec.toml's active `max_lines` clause; its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier ‚Äî both also downstream of that spec.toml commit.
